steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Sentence 2 states that Nora Taylor wanted stations like KBFT to thrive. Sentence 3 states that she founded Native Public Media. The relationship is cause-and-effect: her goal led directly to her action. The transition must signal that the founding was a result of her desire.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Hence,: "Hence" means "as a result" or "therefore" — it signals that what follows is a direct consequence of what came before. Founding Native Public Media is the consequence of Taylor's goal. ✓
  • Choice B — By contrast,: Signals opposition or difference. The founding does not contrast with her goal; it fulfills it. ✗
  • Choice C — In reality,: Signals a correction of a mistaken assumption. Nothing in the passage sets up a misconception to correct. ✗
  • Choice D — In other words,: Signals a restatement or clarification of the same idea. Sentence 3 introduces a new action, not a restatement of sentence 2. ✗
That matches Choice A — Hence,.
